core_mechanism: spectral eigenfunction decomposition of an integral (covariance) operator — a Karhunen-Loève/Mercer expansion — that diagonalizes a correlated random field into a truncated sum of orthogonal, statistically independent modes ranked by eigenvalue
brief_reason: The method represents a Gaussian random field via a KL expansion whose basis functions and weights are the eigenpairs of the integral eigenvalue problem for the covariance kernel, reducing the field to a finite set of independent spectral modes.
